name: Hardkernel ODROID-N2+
description: Amlogic S922X hexa-core SBC with Mali-G52 GPU, mature mid-tier ARM board from Hardkernel

release-date.comment: Announced mid-July 2020; 4GB shipping from July 20, 2020 and 2GB from July 24, 2020.

status.comment: Both 2GB and 4GB variants still listed for sale on Hardkernel as of 2026-04-26; original non-plus N2 is discontinued.

open-source-hardware.comment: Hardkernel publishes schematics PDFs and DXF mechanical drawings only; per RESEARCH.md guidelines, schematics-only does not qualify as OSHW (no PCB design files / Gerbers under an OSHW-compatible license).

country-of-origin.comment: Hardkernel Co., Ltd. is headquartered in South Korea.

soc.comment: S922X Rev. C silicon used on N2+, allowing higher OPP points vs. the original N2.

max-clock-speed-ghz.comment: Cortex-A73 cluster up to 2.4 GHz; A53 cluster runs up to 2.0 GHz.

gpu.comment: Mali-G52 with 6 execution engines per Hardkernel; CNX-Software cites '@ 846MHz', Hardkernel page cites 800 MHz.

opengl-es-version.comment: Mali-G52 IP supports OpenGL ES 3.2 per ARM.

vulkan-support.comment: Mali-G52 IP supports Vulkan 1.x; Hardkernel/Panfrost community has shown working Vulkan stacks on N2+ via mainline (Panfrost/PanVK) and ARM's vendor blob.

npu.comment: S922X has no dedicated NPU; the related Amlogic A311D variant adds an NPU but the N2+ uses S922X.

hw-video-decode.comment: AVE-10 decodes H.264, H.265 (incl. 10-bit), VP9 up to 4K. No AV1 hardware decode on S922X.

hw-video-encode.comment: Independent encoder supports H.264/H.265 up to 1080p60 (plus JPEG).

ram-max-gb.comment: Only 2GB and 4GB variants are sold; no 8GB N2+ has been released.

emmc.comment: eMMC module socket; Hardkernel sells 8GB-256GB modules separately.

m2-nvme-slot.comment: No M.2 slot; NVMe only via USB 3.0 enclosure.

wifi.comment: No onboard Wi-Fi; USB dongle required.

bluetooth.comment: No onboard Bluetooth; USB dongle required.

usb-a-2-ports.comment: USB 2.0 OTG is exposed as a micro-USB port, not a USB-A; counted as 0 USB-A 2.0.

poe-support.comment: No PoE on the Ethernet PHY; no official Hardkernel PoE HAT for N2+.

max-display-resolution.comment: HDMI 2.0 with HDR/CEC/EDID, up to 4K@60Hz; some sources list 4K@75Hz mode.

mipi-dsi.comment: No MIPI-DSI connector on the N2+.

mipi-csi.comment: No MIPI-CSI connector on the N2+.

audio-jack.comment: 3.5mm stereo line-out (combined AV jack with composite video); 384 kHz / 32-bit DAC.

gpio-pins.comment: 40-pin header (25 GPIO usable, plus 2× I2C, SPI, UART, 6× PWM, 2× ADC, S/PDIF).

rpi-compatible-header.comment: 40-pin layout exists but pinout/voltage levels are not Raspberry Pi compatible; some pins are 1.8V.

rpi-hat-compatible.comment: Board is 90×90 mm with custom mounting holes; standard RPi HATs do not fit mechanically or electrically.

pcie-lanes-exposed.comment: S922X has no PCIe; no PCIe pins exposed on the N2+.

i2c-spi-uart.comment: All three (I2C×2, SPI, UART) available on the 40-pin header.

idle-power-w.comment: Hardkernel quotes 2.3–2.5 W idle (no peripherals); used midpoint.

load-power-w.comment: Hardkernel quotes 5.9–6.2 W under stress-ng heavy load; used midpoint.

cooling-required.comment: Ships with a large integrated aluminum heatsink as PCB stiffener; vendor states no fan needed under stock max load.

rtc-battery-header.comment: Onboard RTC IC with CR2032 battery holder.

length-mm.comment: Bare PCB 90×90 mm; 100×91 mm overall with heatsink.

form-factor-class.comment: 90×90 mm square form factor; not Pi-standard, not a recognized ITX class.

weight-g.comment: Includes the integrated aluminum heatsink (the heatsink is not removable in the standard product).

mainline-linux-kernel.comment: Mainline U-Boot has odroid-n2_defconfig; mainline Linux supports the board (Meson G12B DTS, Ethernet, USB3, eMMC, HDMI, GPIO). Armbian ships stable images on kernel 6.18.x (April 2026). Some video codec acceleration paths still rely on V4L2-M2M drivers under active development.

official-os-image.comment: Hardkernel publishes Ubuntu (20.04/22.04 LTS) and Android images.

distros-with-images.comment: Hardkernel publishes Ubuntu directly; Armbian publishes maintained Ubuntu and Debian board images. No board-specific Fedora/openSUSE/Alpine images officially listed.

bootloader.comment: Mainline U-Boot supports the board via odroid-n2_defconfig.

long-term-support.comment: Released July 2020 and still in production with active Hardkernel and Armbian support 5+ years on; Hardkernel does not publish a formal end-of-life date but maintains software updates while the product is sold. Conservatively tagged 3-5y for the remaining window.

android-support.comment: Official Android image (Android 9 Pie) maintained by Hardkernel; community Android 11 builds also exist.

msrp-base-usd.comment: Hardkernel official price for the 2GB variant, checked 2026-04-26.

msrp-top-usd.comment: Hardkernel official price for the 4GB variant, checked 2026-04-26. Earlier launch pricing was $79 (4GB) / $63 (2GB) per CNX-Software (2020); current Hardkernel pricing is higher.

available-regions.comment: Sold direct by Hardkernel (Korea), via ameriDroid (US), and EU resellers (e.g., odroid.nl, Pollin). Snapshot 2026-04-26.

typical-lead-time.comment: Snapshot 2026-04-26: listed as orderable on Hardkernel and ameriDroid without backorder notice.

documentation-quality.comment: Comprehensive official wiki: hardware section with schematics PDFs, mechanical drawings, S922X datasheet link, getting-started, OS images. Loses one point because Hardkernel does not publish PCB design files and some advanced topics (codec stacks, mainline pitfalls) live mainly on the forum.

community-size.comment: ODROID forum has long-running, vendor-blessed activity. RESEARCH.md tier: ODROID listed as 'large'.

active-forum-discord.comment: Vendor-run forum.odroid.com with daily activity and dedicated N2/N2+ subforum.

accessory-ecosystem.comment: Hardkernel sells official cases, eMMC modules, RTC batteries, IO Shield, fan, USB-UART kit, official displays. Limited third-party HAT ecosystem because the GPIO header is not RPi pin-compatible.
